semantic_notes: Column names do not clearly identify either ticker symbols or derived financial metrics.
Review the uploaded headers before using this result as an external benchmark.

notes: Classical and quantum candidates are derived from the same screened asset universe and portfolio constraints.
Dataset semantics do not support a real market benchmark, so this run should be presented only as workflow evidence.
The classical baseline is exact enumeration over the screened universe.
Runtime comparison is descriptive only: classical runs locally in-process, while quantum includes local QAOA search/statevector work plus distributed fragment execution.

semantic_notes: Column names do not clearly identify either ticker symbols or derived financial metrics.
Review the uploaded headers before using this result as an external benchmark.

verdict.headline: This run is strong workflow evidence rather than a quantum-beats-classical result.
verdict.summary: Use this benchmark to demonstrate same-dataset methodology, exact baseline generation, circuit synthesis, and distributed execution evidence. Do not use it as a numerical outperformance claim.
strengths: Classical baseline is exact enumeration across 3 feasible portfolios.
Quantum candidate includes executable OpenQASM with 3 qubits and depth 12.
Quantum execution returned runtime evidence via 17 routed fragments across 10 nodes.
Quantum search assigned 100.00% probability mass to budget-feasible portfolios.
Best feasible quantum state lies on the exact efficient frontier.
limitations: Dataset columns look like derived company metrics rather than tradable securities, so this is not a real portfolio benchmark.
Quantum candidate did not beat the classical objective (+0.108816 vs +0.108816).
Quantum end-to-end quantum path was slower (50 ms vs 1 ms).
Current benchmark uses 8 assets or fewer, so this is still a small-scale benchmark.
recommended_claims: Both classical and quantum candidates were generated from the same screened dataset, objective, budget, and risk settings.
The platform converts a finance dataset into an executable quantum circuit and surfaces distributed runtime evidence end-to-end.
Quantum search concentrated material probability mass on feasible portfolios even though it did not beat the exact optimum.
avoid_claims: Do not present this run as a real portfolio benchmark across tradable securities.
Do not claim quantum outperformed the exact classical baseline on this dataset.
Do not claim a wall-clock speed advantage from this run.
Do not extrapolate this result to production-scale portfolio universes.
